supersedes: Earlier same-day (2026-07-08) checkpoint that covered only the single 07-08 day. This revision (sixth run, 15:48Z) covers the full trailing-7-day chart window with proper daily segment medians (n=5 jobs sample per weekday, n=2 for the 07-05 weekend, n=7 reused for 07-08).
note: Real ISO week W28 is 2026-07-06..07-12; still incomplete (Wed elapsed). The sibling history/2026-W28.json is the established BASELINE but its window (2026-06-30..07-07) is mostly W27 — it is misnamed and must NOT be clobbered. When the full W28 completes (>=07-12), write the complete-week W28 file and relabel the baseline to W27. This checkpoint's window (07-02..07-08) intentionally spans the W27 tail + W28-partial because the report chart window is the trailing 7 calendar days, not an ISO week.

windowRunCount.note: successful merge-group runs in 07-02..07-08; excludes 07-04 (weekend, 0 runs) and 07-01 (8th day, outside trailing-7 window, 20 success). Failures counted per day below.

2026-07-08.note: 1 further run in-progress at analysis time

segmentsByDay.note: execMed/webBuildMed/webRunTestsMed/workerRunTestsMed/e2eMed are medians of a per-day jobs-API sample. n=5 for 07-02/03/06/07, n=2 for 07-05, n=7 (reused from prior 07-08 checkpoint) for 07-08. runnerWait is median per-run (sample perceived - sample exec).

weeklySegmentMedians.note: medians across the 22-run 07-02..07-07 jobs sample (07-08 excluded here, it barely shifts them).

comparisonToBaseline.perceivedP50: 422 vs 396 (+6.6%) — under the 10% regression threshold; flat.
comparisonToBaseline.execution: 396.5 vs 401 (-1%) — flat. Critical-path execution did NOT lengthen.
comparisonToBaseline.webRunTestsStep: 114.5 vs 86 (+33%) and workerRunTests 102 vs 80 (+28%). BUT (1) total execution is flat, so the step rise is absorbed off the critical path; (2) baseline's 86/80 came from a 6-run sample that mixed early-week-low (07-01 webRT 66) and late-week-high single samples, biasing it low; (3) the step level PLATEAUED across 07-06/07/08 (webRT 116/120/114 — flat, no ongoing intra-week shift). Reads as gradual test-suite growth + a low baseline sample, not a config regression. Continue as WATCH.
comparisonToBaseline.webBuild: 125 vs 114 (+10%) — turbo-cache-warmth variance (07-07 sample included a 7s warm-cache Build).

intraWeekShiftCheck.rule: >=50% across 3+ consecutive days is actionable in-run
intraWeekShiftCheck.finding: webRunTests step: 07-02=80 -> 07-03=103 -> 07-05=134(n2) -> 07-06=116 -> 07-07=120 -> 07-08=114. Step-up early week then a flat plateau on the three most recent consecutive days (116/120/114). No sustained >=50% consecutive-day acceleration to trace to a specific day/PR; not actionable this run.

flakyNote: Zero retries across all sampled run-tests logs this run: tests-worker 07-03 (job 85005515493) and 07-07 (85644805589), tests-web client 07-07 (85644805409) — none printed a 'Retried tests (N):' block. Consistent with baseline + prior checkpoint (zero-flaky three samples running).

action.reason: No regression (perceived +6.6% <10%, execution/e2e flat); zero flaky; the step-level web/worker run-tests rise vs baseline is explained by test-suite growth + a low baseline sample and did not lengthen the critical path; every recurring slow test was already investigated in prior weeks and found unfit for an autonomous coverage-preserving PR. Ledger empty; no ci-performance PRs to assess.
